# Image Gallery (React JS) – Machine Coding Interview

An **Image Gallery** is one of the most common React interview questions.

### Features Interviewers Usually Ask

✅ Grid Layout

✅ Lightbox Preview

✅ Previous / Next Navigation

✅ Lazy Loading

✅ Search / Filter Images

✅ Infinite Scroll

✅ Upload Images

✅ Responsive Design

✅ Keyboard Navigation

✅ Pagination

---

# Component Design

```txt
App
│
├── SearchBar
├── Gallery
│    ├── ImageCard
│    ├── ImageCard
│    └── ImageCard
│
└── LightboxModal
```

---

# Basic Image Gallery

## Data

```js
const images = [
  {
    id: 1,
    title: "Nature",
    url: "https://picsum.photos/id/10/600/400",
  },
  {
    id: 2,
    title: "Mountain",
    url: "https://picsum.photos/id/20/600/400",
  },
  {
    id: 3,
    title: "Ocean",
    url: "https://picsum.photos/id/30/600/400",
  },
];
```

---

# Image Card

```jsx
function ImageCard({
  image,
  onClick
}) {
  return (
    {image.url} =>
        onClick(image)
      }
      className="image"
    />
  );
}
```

---

# Gallery Component

```jsx
function Gallery({ images, onImageClick }) {
  return (
    <div className="gallery">
      {images.map((image) => (
        <ImageCard key={image.id} image={image} onClick={onImageClick} />
      ))}
    </div>
  );
}
```

---

# Lightbox Modal

```jsx
function Lightbox({ image, onClose }) {
  if (!image) return null;

  return (
    <div className="overlay" onClick={onClose}>
      <div className="modal">
        {image.url}
        <h3>{image.title}</h3>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
```

---

# App Component

```jsx
import { useState } from "react";

export default function App() {
  const [selectedImage, setSelectedImage] = useState(null);

  return (
    <>
      <Gallery images={images} onImageClick={setSelectedImage} />

      <Lightbox image={selectedImage} onClose={() => setSelectedImage(null)} />
    </>
  );
}
```

---

# Responsive CSS Grid

```css
.gallery {
  display: grid;

  grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(250px, 1fr));

  gap: 16px;
}

.image {
  width: 100%;
  height: 250px;

  object-fit: cover;

  cursor: pointer;
}

.overlay {
  position: fixed;
  inset: 0;

  background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.8);

  display: flex;
  justify-content: center;
  align-items: center;
}
```

---

# Search Functionality

Interview favourite.

```jsx
const [search, setSearch] = useState("");
```

Filter:

```jsx
const filteredImages = images.filter((image) =>
  image.title.toLowerCase().includes(search.toLowerCase()),
);
```

Search UI:

```jsx
<input
  value={search}
  onChange={(e) => setSearch(e.target.value)}
  placeholder="Search..."
/>
```

---

# Previous / Next Navigation

```jsx
const [current, setCurrent] = useState(0);
```

Next:

```jsx
setCurrent((prev) => (prev + 1) % images.length);
```

Previous:

```jsx
setCurrent((prev) => (prev === 0 ? images.length - 1 : prev - 1));
```

---

# Lazy Loading

Improves performance.

```jsx
{
  image.url;
}
```

---

# Infinite Scroll Example

Using Intersection Observer:

```jsx
useEffect(() => {
  const observer = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
    if (entries[0].isIntersecting) {
      loadMoreImages();
    }
  });

  observer.observe(loaderRef.current);

  return () => observer.disconnect();
}, []);
```

---

# Image Upload Example

```jsx
function uploadImage(event) {
  const file = event.target.files[0];

  const imageUrl = URL.createObjectURL(file);

  setImages((prev) => [
    ...prev,
    {
      id: Date.now(),
      url: imageUrl,
      title: file.name,
    },
  ]);
}
```

```jsx
<input type="file" accept="image/*" onChange={uploadImage} />
```

---

# Accessibility (Very Important)

```jsx
<img alt={image.title} />
```

Keyboard close:

```jsx
useEffect(() => {
  function handleKeyDown(e) {
    if (e.key === "Escape") {
      onClose();
    }
  }

  window.addEventListener("keydown", handleKeyDown);

  return () => window.removeEventListener("keydown", handleKeyDown);
}, []);
```

---

# Performance Optimisation

### Use React.memo

```jsx
const ImageCard =
  React.memo(
    function ImageCard() {
      ...
    }
  );
```

### Virtualization

For thousands of images:

```txt
react-window
react-virtualized
```

Render only visible items.

---

# Interview Follow-Up Questions

### How to optimise 10,000 images?

```txt
Lazy Loading
Pagination
Infinite Scroll
Virtualization
CDN
Compressed Images
```

---

### How to load images efficiently?

```txt
Intersection Observer
loading="lazy"
WebP
Image CDN
```

---

### How to support image zoom?

```txt
Lightbox
Transform Scale
Pinch Zoom
```

---

### How to support drag-and-drop uploads?

```txt
react-dropzone
```

---

# Senior-Level Answer

> An Image Gallery can be implemented using a responsive CSS Grid to render image thumbnails and a Lightbox component to preview images. Large-scale galleries should use lazy loading, Intersection Observer, and virtualization to improve performance. Additional features like search, filtering, image uploads, infinite scrolling, keyboard navigation, and accessibility support make the gallery production-ready and scalable for enterprise applications.

# Image Gallery – Add Search/Filter + Lightbox Preview

These are the two most common follow-up questions after implementing a basic Image Gallery.

---

# 1. Search Functionality

## Requirement

Suppose we have images:

```js
const images = [
  {
    id: 1,
    title: "Mountain",
    category: "Nature",
    url: "...",
  },
  {
    id: 2,
    title: "Beach",
    category: "Travel",
    url: "...",
  },
  {
    id: 3,
    title: "Forest",
    category: "Nature",
    url: "...",
  },
];
```

User types:

```txt
mou
```

Gallery should show:

```txt
Mountain
```

---

## Search State

```jsx
const [search, setSearch] = useState("");
```

---

## Search Input

```jsx
<input
  type="text"
  placeholder="Search images..."
  value={search}
  onChange={(e) => setSearch(e.target.value)}
/>
```

---

## Filter Images

```jsx
const filteredImages = images.filter((image) =>
  image.title.toLowerCase().includes(search.toLowerCase()),
);
```

---

## Render

```jsx
<Gallery images={filteredImages} />
```

---

# Search Flow

```txt
User Types
      │
      ▼
Update Search State
      │
      ▼
Filter Images
      │
      ▼
Render Matching Images
```

---

# Example

Input:

```txt
For
```

Output:

```txt
Forest
```

---

# 2. Category Filter

Very common interview extension.

---

## Requirement

```txt
All
Nature
Travel
Food
```

---

## State

```jsx
const [selectedCategory, setSelectedCategory] = useState("All");
```

---

## Dropdown

```jsx
<select
  value={selectedCategory}
  onChange={(e) => setSelectedCategory(e.target.value)}
>
  <option>All</option>
  <option>Nature</option>
  <option>Travel</option>
  <option>Food</option>
</select>
```

---

## Filter Logic

```jsx
const filteredImages = images.filter((image) => {
  const searchMatch = image.title.toLowerCase().includes(search.toLowerCase());

  const categoryMatch =
    selectedCategory === "All" || image.category === selectedCategory;

  return searchMatch && categoryMatch;
});
```

---

# Example

Category:

```txt
Nature
```

Results:

```txt
Mountain
Forest
```

---

# Combined Search + Filter

```txt
Category = Nature

Search = Mo
```

Result:

```txt
Mountain
```

---

# 3. Lightbox Preview

Another favourite interview topic.

---

## What is a Lightbox?

When user clicks image:

```txt
Thumbnail
      ↓
Large Fullscreen Preview
```

Example:

```txt
Gallery
 ┌─────┐
 │ Img │
 └─────┘
      ↓ Click

Fullscreen Preview
```

---

# State

```jsx
const [selectedImage, setSelectedImage] = useState(null);
```

Initially:

```txt
selectedImage = null
```

---

# Click Thumbnail

```jsx
{image.url} =>
    setSelectedImage(image)
  }
/>
```

---

# Lightbox Component

```jsx
function Lightbox({ image, onClose }) {
  if (!image) {
    return null;
  }

  return (
    <div className="overlay" onClick={onClose}>
      <div className="lightbox">
        {image.url}

        <h3>{image.title}</h3>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
```

---

# Add To App

```jsx
<Lightbox image={selectedImage} onClose={() => setSelectedImage(null)} />
```

---

# CSS

```css
.overlay {
  position: fixed;
  inset: 0;

  background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.8);

  display: flex;
  justify-content: center;
  align-items: center;
}

.lightbox img {
  max-width: 80vw;
  max-height: 80vh;
}
```

---

# Lightbox Flow

```txt
Click Image
      │
      ▼
Update selectedImage
      │
      ▼
Render Modal Overlay
      │
      ▼
Show Large Preview
```

---

# 4. Previous / Next Navigation

Interviewers usually ask:

> Navigate images inside lightbox.

---

## State

```jsx
const [currentIndex, setCurrentIndex] = useState(0);
```

---

## Previous

```jsx
function previousImage() {
  setCurrentIndex((prev) => (prev === 0 ? images.length - 1 : prev - 1));
}
```

---

## Next

```jsx
function nextImage() {
  setCurrentIndex((prev) => (prev + 1) % images.length);
}
```

---

## Buttons

```jsx
<button
  onClick={previousImage}
>
  Prev
</button>

<button
  onClick={nextImage}
>
  Next
</button>
```

---

# Keyboard Support

Another interview favourite.

---

## Close

```txt
Escape
```

---

## Navigate

```txt
← Previous
→ Next
```

---

```jsx
useEffect(() => {
  function handleKeyDown(event) {
    if (event.key === "Escape") {
      onClose();
    }

    if (event.key === "ArrowLeft") {
      previousImage();
    }

    if (event.key === "ArrowRight") {
      nextImage();
    }
  }

  window.addEventListener("keydown", handleKeyDown);

  return () => window.removeEventListener("keydown", handleKeyDown);
}, []);
```

---

# Architecture

```txt
Image Gallery
       │
       ▼
 Search Input
       │
       ▼
 Category Filter
       │
       ▼
 Filtered Images
       │
       ▼
 Image Grid
       │
       ▼
 Click Thumbnail
       │
       ▼
 Lightbox Modal
       │
 ┌─────┴─────┐
 ▼           ▼

Prev       Next

       ▼
    Close
```

---

# Performance Optimisations

### Search

Use debounce:

```jsx
useDebounce(search, 300);
```

---

### Gallery

```jsx
React.memo(ImageCard);
```

---

### Large Lists

```txt
react-window
react-virtualized
```

---

### Images

&lt;img loading="lazy" /&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

---

# Senior React Interview Answer

> I would implement search by maintaining a query state and filtering images based on title or metadata. Category filters can be combined with search to create a multi-criteria filtering pipeline. For image preview, I would build a Lightbox component that renders conditionally when a thumbnail is selected. The lightbox would support image navigation, keyboard shortcuts (Escape, ArrowLeft, ArrowRight), and overlay click-to-close behaviour. For large galleries, I would add lazy loading, debounced search, infinite scrolling, and virtualization to ensure performance remains smooth with thousands of images.

## 1. Debounced Search Input

Without debouncing:

```txt
r
re
rea
reac
react
```

Triggers 5 API calls.

With debouncing:

```txt
User Stops Typing 300ms
        ↓
Single API Call
```

---

### Custom Hook

```jsx
import { useState, useEffect } from "react";

export function useDebounce(value, delay = 300) {
  const [debouncedValue, setDebouncedValue] = useState(value);

  useEffect(() => {
    const timer = setTimeout(() => {
      setDebouncedValue(value);
    }, delay);

    return () => clearTimeout(timer);
  }, [value, delay]);

  return debouncedValue;
}
```

---

### Gallery Search

```jsx
import { useState } from "react";
import { useDebounce } from "./useDebounce";

function Gallery({ images }) {

  const [search,
    setSearch] =
    useState("");

  const debouncedSearch =
    useDebounce(search, 500);

  const filteredImages =
    images.filter(image =>
      image.title
        .toLowerCase()
        .includes(
          debouncedSearch.toLowerCase()
        )
    );

  return (
    <>
      <input
        placeholder="Search images..."
        value={search}
        onChange={(e) =>
          setSearch(e.target.value)
        }
      />

      <div className="gallery">
        {filteredImages.map(image => (
          {image.url}
        ))}
      </div>
    </>
  );
}
```

---

## Interview Answer

> Debouncing reduces the frequency of expensive operations by delaying execution until the user stops typing. It improves performance, reduces API traffic, and improves UX in search-heavy applications.

---

# 2. Keyboard Navigation in Lightbox

A professional image gallery should support:

```txt
Esc         → Close

← Arrow     → Previous Image

→ Arrow     → Next Image
```

This aligns with accessibility guidance around keyboard-operable dialogs and navigation. [\[OffenderWa...r Customer \| PDF\]](https://persistentsystems.sharepoint.com/sites/extensure479/Shared%20Documents/OffenderWatch%20-%20Know%20Your%20Customer.pdf?web=1), [\[RFP 20-019...ments v0.2 \| PDF\]](https://persistentsystems.sharepoint.com/sites/EIS-SE-UserExperience/IBMShowroom/ReferenceProposals/Coles%20Australia/RFP%2020-019%20MDM%20Part%20A-4-B%20Coles%20Digital%20Accessibility%20Usability%20Requirements%20v0.2.pdf?web=1)

---

### Lightbox Component

```jsx
import {
  useEffect
} from "react";

function Lightbox({
  images,
  currentIndex,
  setCurrentIndex,
  onClose
}) {

  useEffect(() => {

    function handleKeyDown(
      event
    ) {

      switch (event.key) {

        case "Escape":
          onClose();
          break;

        case "ArrowLeft":
          setCurrentIndex(prev =>
            prev === 0
              ? images.length - 1
              : prev - 1
          );
          break;

        case "ArrowRight":
          setCurrentIndex(prev =>
            (prev + 1) %
            images.length
          );
          break;

        default:
          break;
      }
    }

    window.addEventListener(
      "keydown",
      handleKeyDown
    );

    return () =>
      window.removeEventListener(
        "keydown",
        handleKeyDown
      );

  }, []);

  return (
    <div className="overlay">
      <button
        onClick={onClose}
      >
        ✕
      </button>

      <button
        onClick={() =>
          setCurrentIndex(prev =>
            prev === 0
              ? images.length - 1
              : prev - 1
          )
        }
      >
        ◀
      </button>

      {

      <button
        onClick={() =>
          setCurrentIndex(prev =>
            (prev + 1) %
            images.length
          )
        }
      >
        ▶
      </button>
    </div>
  );
}
```

---

### Add Accessibility

```jsx
<div
  role="dialog"
  aria-modal="true"
  aria-label="Image Preview"
>
```

```jsx
<button
  aria-label="Previous Image"
>
```

```jsx
<button
  aria-label="Next Image"
>
```

---

# 3. Infinite Scroll in Gallery

For large galleries:

```txt
1000+
5000+
10000+
```

Avoid:

```txt
Page 1
Page 2
Page 3
```

Use:

```txt
Infinite Scroll
```

A commonly recommended approach is to use the **Intersection Observer API** to observe a sentinel element and load more content when it becomes visible. This pattern is highlighted in both enterprise discussions and modern React examples. [\[I have pub...aries. In \| Viva Engage\]](https://engage.cloud.microsoft/main/threads/eyJfdHlwZSI6IlRocmVhZCIsImlkIjoiMjI5NzkxMDk5ODQ1MDE3NiJ9), [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/matan3sh/infinite-scroll-in-react-with-intersection-observer-3932)

---

## State

```jsx
const [images, setImages] = useState([]);

const [page, setPage] = useState(1);

const [hasMore, setHasMore] = useState(true);
```

---

## Load Images

```jsx
async function fetchImages() {
  const response = await fetch(
    `https://picsum.photos/v2/list?page=${page}&limit=20`,
  );

  const data = await response.json();

  if (!data.length) {
    setHasMore(false);
    return;
  }

  setImages((prev) => [...prev, ...data]);

  setPage((prev) => prev + 1);
}
```

---

## Sentinel Ref

```jsx
const loaderRef = useRef(null);
```

---

## Intersection Observer

```jsx
useEffect(() => {
  const observer = new IntersectionObserver(
    (entries) => {
      const target = entries[0];

      if (target.isIntersecting && hasMore) {
        fetchImages();
      }
    },
    {
      threshold: 0.5,
    },
  );

  if (loaderRef.current) {
    observer.observe(loaderRef.current);
  }

  return () => observer.disconnect();
}, [page, hasMore]);
```

---

## Render

```jsx
<div className="gallery">

  {images.map(image => (
    {image.download_url}
  ))}

  <div
    ref={loaderRef}
  >
    Loading...
  </div>

</div>
```

---

# Execution Flow

```txt
User Scrolls
      │
      ▼
Loader Visible
      │
      ▼
Intersection Observer
      │
      ▼
Fetch Next Page
      │
      ▼
Append Images
      │
      ▼
Render New Images
      │
      ▼
Continue Scrolling
```

---

# Senior React Interview Answer

> For search, I use a debounced input to avoid firing API calls on every keystroke. For the lightbox, I add keyboard support using `Escape`, `ArrowLeft`, and `ArrowRight`, along with `role="dialog"` and `aria-modal` for accessibility. For large image collections, I implement infinite scrolling using the `IntersectionObserver` API instead of scroll listeners because it is more efficient and avoids unnecessary calculations. I also combine lazy loading (`loading="lazy"`), React.memo, and virtualization for galleries containing thousands of images.

# Complete React Image Gallery

### Features Included

✅ Debounced Search

✅ Category Filter

✅ Infinite Scroll (IntersectionObserver)

✅ Lightbox Preview

✅ Keyboard Navigation

✅ Lazy Loading

✅ React.memo Optimization

✅ Accessibility Support

✅ Ready for Virtualization

The infinite-scroll approach below uses an `IntersectionObserver`, which is a common and efficient pattern for loading additional content when a loader element enters the viewport. [\[From White...usually s \| Viva Engage\]](https://engage.cloud.microsoft/main/threads/eyJfdHlwZSI6IlRocmVhZCIsImlkIjoiMzQ3NDM3MTY2ODg2MDkyOCJ9), [\[AI_Intevie...d_00003240 \| PDF\]](https://persistentsystems.sharepoint.com/sites/PersistentLearningandDevelopment/ResponsiveAssets/SLF/SLF%20AI-interview%20reports/HLS-SLF%20AI-interview%20reports/Myriad/AI_Inteview_Evaluation_Tushar%20Rathod_00003240.pdf?web=1)

---

# Project Structure

```txt
src/
│
├── App.jsx
├── components/
│   ├── Gallery.jsx
│   ├── ImageCard.jsx
│   └── Lightbox.jsx
│
├── hooks/
│   └── useDebounce.js
│
└── styles.css
```

---

# useDebounce Hook

```jsx
import { useState, useEffect } from "react";

export function useDebounce(value, delay = 500) {
  const [debouncedValue, setDebouncedValue] = useState(value);

  useEffect(() => {
    const timer = setTimeout(() => {
      setDebouncedValue(value);
    }, delay);

    return () => clearTimeout(timer);
  }, [value, delay]);

  return debouncedValue;
}
```

---

# Image Card

```jsx
import React from "react";

function ImageCard({
  image,
  onClick
}) {
  return (
    {image.download_url} =>
        onClick(image)
      }
    />
  );
}

export default React.memo(
  ImageCard
);
```

---

# Lightbox Component

```jsx
import {
  useEffect
} from "react";

export default function Lightbox({
  images,
  currentIndex,
  setCurrentIndex,
  onClose
}) {
  if (
    currentIndex === null
  ) {
    return null;
  }

  const currentImage =
    images[currentIndex];

  useEffect(() => {

    function handleKeyDown(
      event
    ) {
      switch (event.key) {

        case "Escape":
          onClose();
          break;

        case "ArrowLeft":
          setCurrentIndex(prev =>
            prev === 0
              ? images.length - 1
              : prev - 1
          );

          break;

        case "ArrowRight":
          setCurrentIndex(prev =>
            (prev + 1) %
            images.length
          );

          break;

        default:
          break;
      }
    }

    window.addEventListener(
      "keydown",
      handleKeyDown
    );

    return () =>
      window.removeEventListener(
        "keydown",
        handleKeyDown
      );

  }, []);

  return (
    <div
      className="overlay"
      role="dialog"
      aria-modal="true"
      aria-label="Image Preview"
    >
      <button
        className="close-btn"
        onClick={onClose}
      >
        ✕
      </button>

      <button
        aria-label="Previous Image"
        onClick={() =>
          setCurrentIndex(prev =>
            prev === 0
              ? images.length - 1
              : prev - 1
          )
        }
      >
        ◀
      </button>

      {

      <button
        aria-label="Next Image"
        onClick={() =>
          setCurrentIndex(prev =>
            (prev + 1) %
            images.length
          )
        }
      >
        ▶
      </button>
    </div>
  );
}
```

---

# Gallery Component

```jsx
import { useEffect, useRef, useState } from "react";

import ImageCard from "./ImageCard";

import Lightbox from "./Lightbox";

import { useDebounce } from "../hooks/useDebounce";

export default function Gallery() {
  const [images, setImages] = useState([]);

  const [page, setPage] = useState(1);

  const [selectedIndex, setSelectedIndex] = useState(null);

  const [search, setSearch] = useState("");

  const debouncedSearch = useDebounce(search);

  const loaderRef = useRef(null);

  async function fetchImages() {
    const response = await fetch(
      `https://picsum.photos/v2/list?page=${page}&limit=20`,
    );

    const data = await response.json();

    setImages((prev) => [...prev, ...data]);
  }

  useEffect(() => {
    fetchImages();
  }, [page]);

  useEffect(() => {
    const observer = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
      if (entries[0].isIntersecting) {
        setPage((prev) => prev + 1);
      }
    });

    const loader = loaderRef.current;

    if (loader) {
      observer.observe(loader);
    }

    return () => observer.disconnect();
  }, []);

  const filteredImages = images.filter((image) =>
    image.author.toLowerCase().includes(debouncedSearch.toLowerCase()),
  );

  return (
    <>
      <input
        placeholder="Search image author..."
        value={search}
        onChange={(e) => setSearch(e.target.value)}
      />

      <div className="gallery">
        {filteredImages.map((image, index) => (
          <ImageCard
            key={image.id}
            image={image}
            onClick={() => setSelectedIndex(index)}
          />
        ))}
      </div>

      <div ref={loaderRef}>Loading...</div>

      <Lightbox
        images={filteredImages}
        currentIndex={selectedIndex}
        setCurrentIndex={setSelectedIndex}
        onClose={() => setSelectedIndex(null)}
      />
    </>
  );
}
```

---

# App Component

```jsx
import Gallery from "./components/Gallery";

import "./styles.css";

export default function App() {
  return <Gallery />;
}
```

---

# CSS

```css
.gallery {
  display: grid;

  grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(250px, 1fr));

  gap: 16px;
}

.image {
  width: 100%;
  height: 250px;

  object-fit: cover;

  cursor: pointer;
}

.overlay {
  position: fixed;
  inset: 0;

  background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.9);

  display: flex;
  justify-content: center;
  align-items: center;

  gap: 20px;
}

.lightbox-image {
  max-width: 80vw;
  max-height: 80vh;
}

.close-btn {
  position: absolute;
  top: 20px;
  right: 20px;
}
```

---

# How Keyboard Navigation Works

The lightbox listens for keyboard events:

```txt
Escape     → Close

ArrowLeft  → Previous Image

ArrowRight → Next Image
```

Example:

```txt
Image 1
Image 2
Image 3
```

Current:

```txt
Image 2
```

Press:

```txt
ArrowRight
```

Result:

```txt
Image 3
```

The implementation uses:

```jsx
window.addEventListener("keydown", handleKeyDown);
```

and updates:

```jsx
setCurrentIndex(...)
```

---

# Infinite Scroll Working

Traditional pagination:

```txt
Page 1
Page 2
Page 3
```

Infinite Scroll:

```txt
User Scrolls
      ↓
Loader Visible
      ↓
IntersectionObserver
      ↓
Fetch Next Page
      ↓
Append Images
```

The observer pattern shown above watches a loader element and loads the next page when it becomes visible. [\[From White...usually s \| Viva Engage\]](https://engage.cloud.microsoft/main/threads/eyJfdHlwZSI6IlRocmVhZCIsImlkIjoiMzQ3NDM3MTY2ODg2MDkyOCJ9), [\[AI_Intevie...d_00003240 \| PDF\]](https://persistentsystems.sharepoint.com/sites/PersistentLearningandDevelopment/ResponsiveAssets/SLF/SLF%20AI-interview%20reports/HLS-SLF%20AI-interview%20reports/Myriad/AI_Inteview_Evaluation_Tushar%20Rathod_00003240.pdf?web=1)

---

# Gallery Performance Optimisation with Virtualization

Suppose:

```txt
10,000 images
```

Current gallery renders:

```txt
10,000 DOM nodes
```

Problem:

```txt
Slow scrolling
High memory
Long render times
```

---

## Solution: Virtualization

Use:

```bash
npm install react-window
```

Instead of:

```jsx
images.map(...)
```

Use:

```jsx
import { FixedSizeGrid } from "react-window";
```

### Concept

Without Virtualization:

```txt
10,000 images
↓
10,000 DOM Nodes
```

With Virtualization:

```txt
10,000 images
↓
Only 20–50 visible DOM Nodes
```

---

## Example

```jsx
import { FixedSizeGrid } from "react-window";

<FixedSizeGrid
  columnCount={4}
  columnWidth={250}
  rowCount={Math.ceil(images.length / 4)}
  rowHeight={250}
  width={1000}
  height={600}
>
  {Cell}
</FixedSizeGrid>;
```

---

# Performance Techniques for Interviews

```txt
✅ Debounced Search

✅ React.memo(ImageCard)

✅ Lazy Loading

✅ Infinite Scroll

✅ Intersection Observer

✅ Virtualization (react-window)

✅ CDN Optimized Images

✅ WebP Images

✅ Resize Images Server Side

✅ Avoid Re-rendering Entire Gallery
```

# Senior React Interview Answer

> For production-scale galleries, I combine debounced search, lazy-loaded images, IntersectionObserver-based infinite scrolling, keyboard-accessible lightboxes, and virtualization using `react-window`. Virtualization is the most important optimisation when displaying thousands of images because it renders only visible rows instead of creating thousands of DOM nodes, significantly reducing memory usage and improving scroll performance.
